items.title: Why AI alone cannot fix social problems
items.summary: Even sophisticated AI systems need human support and institutional capacity to succeed.

items.title: Netflix’s AI deal puts the global VFX workforce at risk
items.summary: A startup founded by Ben Affleck, recently acquired by Netflix, could automate the frame-by-frame work done by artists across India, South Korea, and Latin America.

items.title: Bangladesh’s gig workers are stuck in gas lines as Iran-U.S. war strains fuel supply
items.summary: Motorcycles and private cars line up for hours at a filling station in the Tejgaon Trust area of Dhaka, Bangladesh, on [Month Day, Year], as motorists attempt to purchase limited fuel supplies. Despite government assurances of a stable supply, many fuel stations remain closed for extended periods due to insufficient deliveries from depots, resulting in long queues and public inconvenience. (Photo by Sony Ramani/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

items.title: AI is about to make the global e-waste crisis much worse
items.summary: As demand for AI hardware surges, much of the resulting waste will end up in non-Western countries.

items.title: The Mexican security company with a $1.27 billion surveillance empire
items.summary:
Ciudad Juarez, Chihuahua, Mexico, October 8, 2025: 
Staff monitor screens at the C7 command center for the Department of Public Security for the state of Chihuahua in Ciudad Juarez on Wednesday, October 14, 2025.  Photo by Adriana Zehbrauskas for Rest of World

items.title: Voice actors fight to save their livelihoods and local cultures from Hollywood’s AI push
items.summary: Voice AI tools are quickly replacing voice-over and dubbing artists, raising concerns not only about jobs but also loss of cultural relevance in non-English-speaking nations.

items.title: RedNote chases U.S. expansion after its “TikTok refugee” moment fades
items.summary: China's Xiaohongshu has an uphill climb in a saturated market as it hires in the U.S. and launches an e-commerce portal for overseas markets.

items.title: In its push to become Big Tech’s data center hub, India is overlooking local resistance
items.summary: Google and Microsoft’s multibillion-dollar projects under construction in India are facing backlash from farmers, while the government offers huge tax relief to foreign companies setting up data centers.

items.title: Chinese entrepreneurs should go global before they go viral
items.summary: The Meta-Manus deal holds lessons for U.S. investors and Chinese founders alike.

items.title: War in the Gulf could tilt the cloud race toward China
items.summary: Strikes on U.S. data centers highlight the risks of concentration and the growing role of geopolitics in cloud competition.

items.title: A Mexican surveillance giant you’ve never heard of is now watching the U.S. border
items.summary:
Ciudad Juarez, Chihuahua, Mexico, October 8, 2025: 
The Centinela Tower (under construction) on Wednesday, October 14, 2025. The tower, built by Seguritech, will be the hub for the Chihuahua border state’s surveillance apparatus. Photo by Adriana Zehbrauskas for Rest of World

items.title: Winners of the 2026 Photo Contest
items.summary: Photographers all over the world submitted images showcasing unique views of technology.
